html:
In this proposal, the government suggests amendments to the law that introduce specific information requirements for licensees who offer lotteries benefiting certain political organizations. These requirements include providing information about the licensee or an organization that receives support from the licensee, both when offering the lottery and in commercial advertisements about the lottery.

The proposal also suggests amendments to the law that stipulate that lotteries offered by a licensee who is themselves, or who provides support to, certain political organizations should not be subject to the relaxed conditions in gambling regulations that apply to other non-profit lotteries. Consequently, such lotteries will no longer be exempt from gambling tax or from the restrictions on credit and bonuses in the gambling laws.

These changes aim to ensure that consumers receive clear information about the fact that by purchasing a lottery ticket, they may be supporting a political organization, and to eliminate unjustified preferential treatment for politically-oriented lotteries.

The proposed amendments are scheduled to come into effect on January 1, 2026.

Proposed Resolutions for the Parliament

The government's proposal:

  1. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Gambling Act (2018:1138).
  2. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2018:1139) on tax on gambling.
  3. (This number is missing in the original text)
tldr: The government wants to change the rules regarding lotteries that benefit political parties. The aim is to ensure that lottery buyers are clearly informed about where their money is going, and that these lotteries pay more in taxes.
lattlast:
The government has proposed new laws that will change how lotteries supporting political parties operate. Those authorized to sell these lotteries must now be very clear about who receives the money. Information about which party or organization receives the support must be included when purchasing the lottery ticket and in all advertising for the lottery.

Previously, some lotteries had been granted exemptions, meaning they had simpler rules than others. The government now proposes that these political lotteries no longer be granted these exemptions. This means they will no longer be exempt from gambling tax, and they must comply with the same rules as other games regarding credit and bonuses.

The reason for the proposal is to protect lottery buyers. The aim is to ensure that all customers know whether their money is going to a political party. At the same time, the proposal aims to eliminate advantages that are not considered fair to other types of lotteries.

If the parliament approves the proposal, the new rules will come into effect on January 1, 2026. The proposal involves changes to two laws: the Gambling Act and the Gambling Tax Act.
